# python-cli-ui

Tools for nice user interfaces in the terminal.

## Note

This project was originally hosted on the [TankerHQ](https://github.com/TankerHQ) organization, which was my employer from 2016
to 2021. They kindly agreed to give back ownership of this project to
me. Thanks!

## Documentation

See [python-cli-ui documentation](https://your-tools.github.io/python-cli-ui).

## Demo

Watch the [asciinema recording](https://asciinema.org/a/112368).

## Usage

```console
$ pip install cli-ui
```

Example:

```python
import cli_ui

# coloring:
cli_ui.info(
  "This is",
  cli_ui.red, "red", cli_ui.reset,
  "and this is",
  cli_ui.bold, "bold"
)

# enumerating:
list_of_things = ["foo", "bar", "baz"]
for i, thing in enumerate(list_of_things):
    cli_ui.info_count(i, len(list_of_things), thing)

# progress indication:
cli_ui.info_progress("Done",  5, 20)
cli_ui.info_progress("Done", 10, 20)
cli_ui.info_progress("Done", 20, 20)

# reading user input:
with_sugar = cli_ui.ask_yes_no("With sugar?", default=False)

fruits = ["apple", "orange", "banana"]
selected_fruit = cli_ui.ask_choice("Choose a fruit", choices=fruits)

#  ... and more!
```

## Contributing

We use [optimistic merging](https://dmerej.info/blog/post/optimistic-merging/) so you don't have to worry too much about formatting the code, pleasing the linters or making sure all the test pass.

That being said, if you want, you can install [just](https://just.systems/man/en/) and use it to check your changes automatically. Just run `just` to see available tasks.

functions.name: cli_ui.setup
documentation:
Configure behavior of message functions.

```
param verbose

Whether  messages should get printed

param quiet

Hide every message except , , and


param color

Choices: 'auto', 'always', or 'never'. Whether to color output.
By default ('auto'), only use color when output is a terminal.

param title

Ditto for setting terminal title

param timestamp

Whether to prefix every message with a time stamp
```

functions.name: cli_ui.process_tokens
documentation:
Returns two strings from a list of tokens.
One containing ASCII escape codes, the other
only the 'normal' characters

functions.name: cli_ui.message
documentation: Helper method for error, warning, info, debug

functions.name: cli_ui.fatal
documentation:
Print an error message and exit the program

```
param tokens

list of  constants or strings, like
(cli_ui.red, "this is a fatal  error")

param exit_code

value of the exit code (default: 1)
```

functions.name: cli_ui.error
documentation: Print an error message

functions.name: cli_ui.warning
documentation: Print a warning message

functions.name: cli_ui.info
documentation:
Print an informative message

```
param tokens

list of  constants or strings, like (cli_ui.red, 'this is an error')

param sep

separator, defaults to ' '

param end

token to place at the end, defaults to '\n'

param fileobj

file-like object to print the output, defaults to sys.stdout

param update_title

whether to update the title of the terminal window
```

documentation: Print an underlined section name

functions.name: cli_ui.info_2
documentation: Print an not so important informative message

functions.name: cli_ui.info_3
documentation: Print an even less important informative message

functions.name: cli_ui.dot
documentation:
Print a dot without a newline unless it is the last one.

Useful when you want to display a progress with very little
knowledge.

```
param last

whether this is the last dot (will insert a newline)
```

functions.name: cli_ui.info_count
documentation:
Display a counter before the rest of the message.

`rest` and `kwargs` are passed to {func}`info`

Current index should start at 0 and end at `n-1`, like in `enumerate()`

```
param i

current index

param n

total number of items
```

functions.name: cli_ui.info_progress
documentation:
Display info progress in percent.

```
param value

the current value

param max_value

the max value

param prefix

the prefix message to print
```

functions.name: cli_ui.debug
documentation:
Print a debug message.

Messages are shown only when `CONFIG["verbose"]` is true

functions.name: cli_ui.tabs

functions.name: cli_ui.message_for_exception
documentation:
Returns a tuple suitable for cli_ui.error()
from the given exception.
(Traceback will be part of the message, after
the `message` argument)

Useful when the exception occurs in an other thread
than the main one.

functions.name: cli_ui.ask_choice
documentation:
Ask the user to choose from a list of choices.

Will loop until:
: - the user enters a valid index
  - or leaves the prompt empty

In the last case, `None` will be returned

```
param prompt

a list of tokens suitable for 

param choices

a list of arbitrary elements

param func_desc

a callable. It will be used to display and
sort the list of choices (unless sort is False)
Defaults to the identity function.

param sort

whether to sort the list of choices.

return

the selected choice.
```

functions.name: cli_ui.select_choices
documentation:
Ask the user to select one or multiple from a list of choices,
delimited by space, comma or semi colon.

Will loop until:

> - the user enters a valid selection
> - or leaves the prompt empty

In the last case, `None` will be returned

```
param prompt

a list of tokens suitable for 

param choices

a list of arbitrary elements

param func_desc

a callable. It will be used to display and
sort the list of choices (unless sort is False)
Defaults to the identity function.

param sort

whether to sort the list of choices.

return

the selected choice(s).
```

functions.name: cli_ui.ask_yes_no
documentation: Ask the user to answer by yes or no

functions.name: cli_ui.did_you_mean
documentation:
Given a list of choices and an invalid user input, display the closest
items in the list that match the input.
